channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🦠 WHO Declares Ebola Global Health Emergency in Congo-Uganda Outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern after more than 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths. The CDC confirmed Americans are among those affected.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Earthquake Kills Two, Forces Thousands to Evacuate
item.description: A magnitude 5.2 earthquake in China's southwest Guangxi region killed two people and forced more than 7,000 in the city of Liuzhou to evacuate. Authorities warn of transport disruptions as search and rescue operations continue.

item.title: 📉 Global Bond Rout Deepens as Iran War Fuels Inflation Fears
item.description: Bonds from Tokyo to New York extended losses as rising energy prices from the ongoing Middle East war fanned inflation fears and stoked investor wagers on rate hikes from global central banks. The yield spike poses risks to unprepared equity markets.

item.title: 💰 Iran War Costs Global Companies $25 Billion and Counting
item.description: A review of corporate statements since the start of the conflict by companies listed in the United States, Europe and Asia reveals the sobering financial fallout, with losses mounting as the war disrupts supply chains and energy markets.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President Defends US Arms Purchases After Trump Questions Support
item.description: Taiwan's president says arms purchases from the United States are crucial for regional stability, after President Donald Trump questioned continued U.S. support for Taiwan following his visit to China.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuba Seeks Russian Drones, Eyes Guantanamo Attack
item.description: US intelligence reports Cuba has purchased 300+ Russian and Iranian drones and is discussing attacks on Guantanamo Bay and Key West, with Iranian military advisors now in Havana.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish Arms Subsidiary Rebrands After Israel Sales Backlash
item.description: Repkon USA, a Turkish-owned defense contractor, rebranded to Paligen Technologies after revelations it would supply bomb bodies for Israel, sparking protests in Istanbul and parliamentary inquiries in Turkey.

item.title: 🇪🇹 Ethiopia-Eritrea War Fears as Tigray Tensions Escalate
item.description: TPLF leader Debretsion Gebremichael reclaimed presidency of Tigray region, challenging the interim administration. Ethiopian fighter jets seen over Mekelle as fears grow of renewed conflict with Eritrea.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky Touts Shift in Battlefield Balance After Moscow Strike
item.description: Following Ukraines largest drone attack on Moscow in over a year, Zelensky announced a shift in momentum and promised more deep strikes as Russia downed over 3,000 drones in the past week.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary Opposition Leads Orbán for First Time in 15 Years
item.description: Péter Magyars Tisza party leads Viktor Orbáns Fidesz by 12 points ahead of April elections in what Politico calls the EUs most consequential election of 2026.

item.title: 🌊 Climate Change Reducing Oxygen Levels in Rivers Worldwide
item.description: A new study reveals that global warming is systematically reducing dissolved oxygen in rivers across the globe, creating expanding "dead zones" that threaten aquatic ecosystems and drinking water supplies.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Economic Growth Stalls as Output and Retail Sales Miss Forecasts
item.description: Industrial output and retail sales sharply undershot expectations in April as the world second-biggest economy wrestled with higher energy costs from the Iran war and persistently weak domestic demand.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Climb After Drone Attack on UAE Nuclear Plant
item.description: Oil prices extended gains as efforts to end the US-Iran conflict appeared stalled following a drone attack on a UAE nuclear power plant and President Trump prepared to discuss military options on Iran.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German Prosecutors Expose Russian Sanctions-Busting Network
item.description: A four-year investigation uncovered a Lübeck-based trading firm that routed dual-use components through Turkey into Russia, violating Western sanctions to supply the Kremlin war machine.

item.title: 🛢️ Brent Crude Breaks $111 as Gulf Drone Attacks Spark Shortage Fears
item.description: Oil prices surged in early Asian trading after drone strikes on UAE and Saudi Arabia, combined with failed Iran diplomacy, raised fears of global supply disruptions. WTI hit $108.20 while Brent climbed to $111.50.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 Xi and Trump Agree Iran Must Not Obtain Nuclear Weapons
item.description: China and the US reached a rare point of agreement on Iran policy during Trumps China visit, with both leaders signaling that Tehrans nuclear ambitions must be contained, reshaping Middle East power dynamics.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Blames Iran for Drone Strike Near Barakah Nuclear Power Plant
item.description: The United Arab Emirates accused Iran or its proxies of launching a drone that sparked a fire just outside the Barakah nuclear facility. No radiation leaks were reported, but the incident marks a dangerous escalation as Iran war ceasefire talks remain stalled.

item.title: 🇺🇸 NATO Leaders Question US Reliability Under Trump Amid Ukraine Security Concerns
item.description: European allies express growing uncertainty about American commitment to collective defense as Trump signals willingness to reduce US military presence in Europe and press Ukraine to accept territorial concessions.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Reports Drone Strike Caused Fire at Barakah Nuclear Plant
item.description: Abu Dhabi confirmed a drone strike sparked a fire at the Barakah nuclear power plant. No injuries or radiation leaks reported. The attack comes as Iran war cease-fire negotiations remain at an impasse.

item.title: 🌍 G7 Finance Chiefs Tackle Trade Imbalances Amid Global Strains
item.description: Group of Seven finance ministers meeting in Rome face mounting pressure to address global trade imbalances and market strains as the Iran war disrupts energy markets and supply chains.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Establishes Defense Offices in Former UNRWA Compound
item.description: Israel converted a former UNRWA headquarters in East Jerusalem into defense ministry offices, a move critics call de facto annexation. The compound had housed UN refugee agency operations for decades.

item.title: ⚔️ Russia Destroyed Over 3,000 Ukrainian Drones Last Week
item.description: Russia downed 3,124 Ukrainian drones in the past week, including over 1,000 targeting Moscow, as Kyiv launched its largest drone offensive of the war. Three civilians were killed in the Moscow region attacks.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Kremlin Signals Interest in Restoring Dialogue With Europe
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov indicated Russia would welcome renewed diplomatic channels with European nations after Ukraine launched its largest drone attack on Moscow in over a year.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Ditches Oil, AI to Build Bridge to Global South
item.description: The Emirates is positioning itself as a neutral mediator between West and Global South, investing heavily in AI infrastructure while moving away from hydrocarbon dependency.
